articles.title: China's Xi meets Taiwan opposition leader ahead of key summit with Trump
articles.summary: Xi Jinping and the KMT's Cheng Li-wun agreed to pursue peace, but Taiwan's ruling party worries it will enable Beijing to undermine its democracy.

articles.title: The Mass Purge of Immigration Judges, and Melania Trump’s Surprise Epstein Remarks
articles.summary: Plus, your Friday news quiz.

articles.title: The Oil Shock Is Worse Than You Think
articles.summary: The war with Iran is preventing huge amounts of oil from flowing out of the Persian Gulf, but the prices that many people track don’t fully capture the scale of the disruption.

articles.title: 'How are you using AI?' Your therapist should ask you that question, experts argue
articles.summary: A paper in JAMA Psychiatry says mental health providers should ask if patients are using artificial intelligence chatbots, just as they would ask patients about sleep habits and substance use.

articles.title: Inflation surges to highest level in nearly two years as energy costs spike
articles.summary: Consumer prices in March were up 3.3% from a year ago, the biggest annual increase in nearly two years.  Higher gasoline prices tied to the war with Iran accounted for much of the surge.

articles.title: VP Vance to lead U.S. team in Iran peace talks. And, Artemis II to return to Earth
articles.summary: Vice President Vance is heading to Pakistan to lead a U.S. team in talks aimed at ending the war in Iran. And, Artemis II is set to return to Earth today.

articles.title: Trump says Iran 'doing a very poor job' in reopening the Strait of Hormuz
articles.summary: The fragile ceasefire agreement was tested again on Friday after Iran refused to reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Israel and Hezbollah traded strikes in Lebanon, and Kuwait was attacked with drones.

articles.title: With Iran Setting Limits, Strait of Hormuz Remains Thorny Politically
articles.summary: Even after a cease-fire, Iran is keeping a chokehold on traffic, forcing countries to cut deals that could put them at odds with the U.S.

articles.title: India cracks down on social media jokes criticizing prime minister
articles.summary: As criticism of Prime Minister Narendra Modi's response to the Iran war grows, government censors are scrambling to knock down memes that use the Indian leader as the punchline.

articles.title: Communities are waiting on billions in disaster funding from the Trump administration
articles.summary: States say disaster funding from the Federal Emergency Management Agency has slowed to a trickle under the Trump administration. That's delaying projects to protect communities from wildfires and hurr

articles.title: Vance Faces Test of His Negotiating Skills With Iran Talks
articles.summary: Vice President JD Vance is leading negotiations this weekend toward an end to a war that he had opposed starting.

articles.title: Consumer Spending, Engine of the U.S. Economy, Is Under Strain
articles.summary: Higher fuel costs are raising food and travel prices, while a shaky stock market tamps down free spenders.

articles.title: Stephen Colbert and Marjorie Taylor Greene are OUT. Who is IN? The quiz knows
articles.summary: If you keep up with secret identities, you'll get at least one question right this week!

articles.title: Women are getting most of the new jobs. What's going on with men?
articles.summary: Over the past year, the vast majority of new jobs have gone to women. One economist says to help men find work, we need to embrace ways to "make girly jobs appeal to manly men."

articles.title: How Airlines Turned First-Class Seats From Freebies to a Profit Engine
articles.summary: Airlines used to give away most of their nicest seats, but they have increasingly found ways to persuade people to pay a lot for them.

articles.title: Nations tried to impose a digital fog of war in Iran. The results are mixed
articles.summary: Governments are blocking the internet, banning social media posts and cutting access to commercial satellite images. But experts say that efforts to censor information have had mixed results.

articles.title: War in Iran strands tons of tea in Kenyan port
articles.summary: Iran war-linked shipping disruptions have stranded 8 million kg of tea in the Kenyan port of Mombasa, costing the industry about $8 million a week in mounting losses.

articles.title: Israel and Lebanon to hold ceasefire talks
articles.summary: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to hold direct ceasefire talks for the first time in decades in a bid to halt the violence in southern Lebanon.

articles.title: Hungary's ruling party accuses Ukraine of sabotage as it readies for crucial election
articles.summary: Ukraine is playing an outsized role in Hungary's election, where the Kremlin-aligned ruling party accuses Kyiv of sabotaging pipelines, laundering money and dragging Hungarians into war.

articles.title: Tracking 7 of Mamdani’s Biggest Campaign Promises
articles.summary: Zohran Mamdani based his successful campaign for mayor of New York City on several central pledges. Here’s an early look on where those stand.

articles.title: Melania Trump Says She Was Not Associated With Jeffrey Epstein
articles.summary: Responding to what she said were smears, the first lady said she never had knowledge of Jeffrey Epstein’s abuse and was not a victim of his. She called for a congressional hearing for his victims.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
